title: Scandix bulbosa
intro: Scandix bulbosa, also known as wild celery or shepherd's needle, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The health benefits of Scandix bulbosa include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Scandix bulbosa is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, flu, and digestive disorders.
taxonomy: Scandix bulbosa is classified as a species of plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This classification places it within the order Apiales and the clade asterids. Within the family Apiaceae, Scandix bulbosa is placed in the subfamily Apioideae. The genus Scandix is also a part of the family Apiaceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of Europe and Asia.
